L1.head: iCASE strategy implementation
L1.claim: iCASE strategy implementation facilitates adaptive structural responses

L1.head: adaptive structural responses
L1.tail: enzyme interactions with substrates and reaction environments
L1.claim: Adaptive structural responses optimize enzyme interactions with substrates and reaction environments

L1.head: optimized enzyme interactions
L1.claim: Optimized enzyme interactions enhance thermal stability and catalytic activity

L2.head: Integration of structure-based supervised machine learning models with molecular dynamics simulations
L2.tail: accurate prediction of binding modes of polyethylene terephthalate hydrolases with polymers
L2.claim: Integration of ML models and MD simulations enables accurate prediction of PET hydrolases' binding modes

L2.head: accurate prediction of binding modes
L2.tail: design of enzymes with enhanced specificity and activity towards PET degradation
L2.claim: Accurate binding mode prediction enables design of PET-degrading enzymes with enhanced specificity

L2.head: molecular response mechanism derived from adaptive evolution models
L2.claim: Adaptive evolution-derived mechanisms inform targeted enzyme engineering

L2.head: targeted enzyme engineering strategies
L2.claim: Targeted engineering strategies exploit epistatic interactions

L2.head: structural resilience
L2.tail: improved thermal stability and catalytic efficiency
L2.claim: Enhanced structural resilience improves thermal stability and catalytic efficiency
